posts.author: SWRS
posts.content:
[Predictions]

Della Maddalena vs. Prates
Jack Della Maddalena, TKO, R4

posts.author: humble
posts.content:
[Predictions]

Della Maddalena vs. Prates
Carlos Prates, TKO, R2
WE all want JDM to win but I think Prates is just too dangerous. The knees and leg kicks are gonna be here and stifle a lot of JDM's game. I think Garry had the much better physical attributes to implement a gameplan against Prates and even then he was on his ass crawling around in the 5th. Prates' volume has only been getting better since then and his power can't be overstated.

posts.author: KMPdoesUFC
posts.content:
[Predictions]

Look this is a sick fight and amazing matchmaking on the UFC's part after a rocky first start to the year, this is an amazing fight night, but my issue is that if Carlos Prates beats JDM he is 100% the number 1 contender and a title shot would be next for Prates, however if JDM gets the job done and solidifies a defence, how likely is it that he's gonna get a rematch over Islam or if by some miracle the champ is Ian Garry by then, my point is invalid.

posts.author: elitemmapicks
posts.content:
[Predictions]

Jack Della Maddalena vs Carlos Prates
Pick: Della Maddalena via decision
59% of Tapology users (213 users) are picking Della Maddalena (71% decision)
Della Maddalena is 3.1 years younger (29.7 vs 32.7)
Prates has 5” reach edge (78” vs 73”)
Della Maddalena has +1.73 strike differential/min vs -0.76
Della Maddalena has +2.07 strike differential/min @distance vs -1.94
Della Maddalena proj. +27.9 significant strikes attempted
Della Maddalena proj. +27.5 significant strikes landed
Della Maddalena proj. +14.2 head strikes landed
Della Maddalena has 47% head strike accuracy, 71% head strike defense
Prates has 44% head strike accuracy, 59% head strike defense
Della Maddalena has 12 pt edge in HSD, Prates has 3 pt edge in HSA
Prates proj. 1.88 knockdowns vs 0.57
Della Maddalena proj. 41.7% finish chance vs 39.8% and 18.6% decision
Good upside on Della Maddalena via KO (decision is safer play)
7/12 KO’s for Della Maddalena were in round 1
4/12 KO’s for Della Maddalena were in round 2
Prates has 18:2 KO ratio (both times KO’d were in round 1)
Della Maddalena will likely win striking, head strike battle

posts.author: Xion
posts.content:
[Predictions]

Dariush vs. Salkilld
Quillan Salkilld, TKO, R1
Damn man. I love Beneil Dariush bro why we gotta do this to unc. Literally being sacrificed here.

Tuivasa vs. Sharaf
Tai Tuivasa, TKO, R1
SURELY THIS IS A FREE BAM BAM WIN SURELY

Elliott vs. Erceg
Steve Erceg, DEC
Great fight and decent matchmaking for once in the UFC this fight actually makes sense. I'm picking Erceg to pretty comfortably win this fight. Outboxes and out grapples Tim Elliot and shows he is high level once again. I know I KNOW Ode Osbourne rocked his **** but tbf he hits really hard and Stevey has a sus chin but still he ate it and beat his ass. I think Erceg's gonna have an answer for everything Elliot's gonna throw at him.

Malkoun vs. Meerschaert
Jacob Malkoun, TKO, R2
**** Malkoun looked incredible in his last fight. Watched that one in person and HE SHOULD'VE GOTTEN A ******* BONUS. Anyway it doesn't matter because paramount doesn't care about the athletes or the sport even. Malkoun chins GM3 and looks like a world champion in the process. Dude looks in peak form right now and i like him getting a few good wins and potentially moving up in the rankings soon.

Tafa vs. Christian
Junior Tafa, TKO, R1
Kevin Christian should not be in the UFC right now. Tbf neither should Junior Tafa but he is pretty good on the feet and KO's most unranked guys. Like this one. R1 KO for The Juggernaut. Almost had his moment against Elekana in Sydney but the Tafa bros cant grapple to save their lives and it's very unfortunate.

Rowston vs. Bryczek
Cam Rowston, TKO, R3
Great fight i feel like we haven't seen Bryczek in a while. Cam Rowston is a nasty mfer and has a clean fighting style and actually looks for the finish. Active on the feet and can defend a takedown and looks like he definitely knows what he's doing on the ground. TKO for Cam Rowston later in the fight R2 or 3. Finds the range later on when Bryczek gasses deeper into the fight.

Steele vs. Mar Fan
Dom Mar Fan, TKO, R3
Damn i just KNOW this is FOTN. These ******* guy are gonna go to war bro i can't wait. Dom Mar Fan really impresses me with the improvements he's made in his game recently and he shows so much championship mettle and potential. Kody Steele has some gritty and nasty boxing and just wants to tough it out with you and DMF is 100% more than happy to oblige. I think i like him better in the one just because he'll have an outside speed advantage and is more technical with his striking setups and has some solid defence and a ******* iron chin to go with it. I like a TKO in the fight against the fence late for Street Buddha just because of Steele's reckless nature and he'll get a bit gassed and DMF will take over by the 2nd round. R3 TKO for Dom Mar Fan. I guarantee this fight will be a certified hood classic.

posts.author: Goat******76
posts.content:
[Predictions]

Sweet Mary Mother of ******* Christ…

I have seen some utterly repugnant, heinously anus matchmaking in the UFC before, but seeing Tai Tuivasa vs Sean Sharaf as an honest-to-god real fight feels like an April Fools joke that’s a week premature. For some indescribable reason, the UFC wants to cling their hands to Tai’s pudgy waist so desperately that they not only dismiss his loss streak entirely as a very concrete reason to justifiably cut him from the roster, they gave him Sean ******* Sharaf. Sean is not a can, he the can of cans, the slobbiest of heavyweight slobs. He’s the equivalent of the fat chick in the group of popular mean girls in a high school: he exists solely to make other people look better. If a UFC contender’s skillset could be compared to an IRL profession, he is the skill equivalent of a **** set **** mopper. To go from fighting Cyril Gane to a human sceptic tank is such an embarrassing drop in quality competition wise that if I were in Tai’s shoes (preferably not the ones filled with ******* Blue Moon) I would hang up the gloves, immediately. GOD ******* FORBID he loses this fight somehow. He needs this more than Ben Shapiro needs a pack of tampons.

Tai via hopefully R1 KO

* Edited at 03.25.2026,  8:11 PM ET *

posts.author: A_Person_05
posts.content:
[Predictions]

Dariush vs. Salkilld
Quillan Salkilld, TKO, R1
Dariush has no chin anymore. Moicano dropped him bad. BSD lightly tapped him on the temple and he died. Arman knocked him out with a shot on one foot. Salkilld has big power, range, grappling defense, etc. Against Moicano Dariush won by outpointing him with kicks, and reversing grappling exchnages into advantageous positions. Salkilld isn't gonna get outpointed with kicks, he isn't going to shoot bad takedowns because he is losing at kickboxing. He will keep it on the feet and hit Dariush with something that turns him off.

Tuivasa vs. Sharaf
Tai Tuivasa, TKO, R1
If you lost to a Tafa get out of the sport. Even Tai maybe wins this. Who cares?

Elliott vs. Erceg
Steve Erceg, DEC
Erceg out pointing his way to a decision. Erceg can't beat these elite FLW strikers, and also can't outgrapple them, but he can outsrike the mid FLW grapplers and use his counter grappling and wrestling to keep it standing or reverse positions.

Malkoun vs. Meerschaert
Jacob Malkoun, TKO, R1
Meerschaert is done. It's over. Be happy that it happened, not sad that it is over. It was magical. I really hope he has one more come from behind late round submission in his bag. I just watched his last fights, and it's over.

Tafa vs. Christian
Kevin Christian, DEC
Tafa sucks.

Rowston vs. Bryczek
Cam Rowston, TKO, R1
Rowstan is good Bryczek is not.

Steele vs. Mar Fan
Kody Steele, SUB, R2
Kody Steele my WAsian goat. This will be a banger, it's a sleeper matchup. Here is what I think it comes down to, Fan is not a finisher, every Road fight was a decision. Steele finishes people or dies trying, and I don't think Fan can put him out. Steele also has an insane BJJ background but scraps on the feet (think Gregory Rodrigues), I think he hurts Fan on the feet and then subs him on the ground.

Micallef vs. Gorimbo
Jonathan Micallef, TKO, R1
Micallef is good... Gorimbo... is not.

Gaziev vs. Peričić
Shamil Gaziev, DEC
Gaziev title walk ensuing.

Thicknesse vs. Morales
Colby Thicknesse, DEC
To be clear Thicknesse is not good. He got overrated to all hell by Aussie fans and delusional haters that claimed he beat Topuria Sr. With all that said feeding him Morales is a nice way to get him a win before he loses to someone else.

Schultz vs. Johnston
Ben Johnston, TKO, R2
Didn't watch Schultz on DWCS, watched the fight where Pinas busted shots all over him, Schultz looked like **** and got walked over. Looked like middleweight Ryan Hall, but specifically the Ryan Hall that fought Topuria.

Jenkins vs. Rahiki
Jack Jenkins, DEC
Jenkins is really good, he hits the legs constantly, he has solid boxing, and he has nasty combinations. Rahiki looked good in his debut, if he wins this I am on the hype train, but Jenkins has such a unique combination of skills I think its a really tough matchup for someone's second UFC fight.

posts.author: zeddhero
posts.content:
[Predictions]

Della Maddalena vs Prates
Pick: Jack Della by Decision
Jack Della Maddalena is a very, very, very good fighter and I think too many are forgetting how skilled he is because of that Islam fight. Yes, Islam wrestle-f*cked him the whole fight, yes, Islam got the better of some of the striking exchanges as well, but here Prates isn't even going to try to exploit some of Jack's weakeness on the ground. If anything, I think Jack may intiate some of the grappling exchanges later on. Prates has power with that left straight, he's put so many fighters out with it, but this is the type of fight Jack is made for. His top tier boxing, head movement, combos and IQ is going to shine big here, I could honestly see a masterclass from him here. These two have granite chins (especially Jack omfg) so doubt either man finishes the other, but Jack is going to have more moments and more strikes landed in order to win this one. Can't wait for him to remind the fans just how good he is.

Dariush vs. Salkilld
Pick: Beneil Dariush by Decision
I get Salkilld is very very good and is a very promising prospect, but after seeing some of the trends lately in the LW division, and how good Moicano looked against Duncan. I'm starting to think there's still levels to this ****. Dariush's chin is made of glass for sure, but this guy's grappling and wrestling is still very slick, and rewatching some of the exchanges with BSD, he did land a couple of good shots before he got dropped and put out. If Salkilld doesn't put Benny away early, Benny is going to take over and show how valuable his experience truly is. I do like Salkilld a lot, perhaps my favorite LW prospect and this is a semi-favorable matchup, but I just think Benny is gonna win the grappling exchanges and outlast Salkilld to win a 29-28.

* Edited at 04.05.2026, 10:42 AM ET *

posts.author: McFarlanders
posts.content:
[Predictions]

Dariush dec
Yeah sure, his chin is now composed mostly of styrofoam, but he’s a crafty man. The only reason he’s 1-3 in his last four is because his opponents have decent or huge power in their hands. Oliveria and Tsarukyan are the cream of the crop of LW, no shame in losing to them. BSD is a marauder who has nasty hands, a LOT of people would get colded had BSD not have the throwing speed of a slug. So what does Salkilld have in order to beat Benny? A finish over the Indian (look at him, he looks chinny), the Israeli (thats all I know abt him), and Nasrat (a decent win that is his best win). But Salkilld is going against the man who schooled Gamer man Gamrot and Money Moicano, two other crafty vets. I think its too early for Salkilld, as he’s someone I don’t think has the power to KO Benny nor has the skills to outgame him. Henceforth, it will be a Benny win by 30-27, 30-27, 29-28 Unanimous decision.

posts.author: Josh7
posts.content:
[Predictions]

Della Maddalena vs. Prates
Carlos Prates, TKO, R3
I can't go against Prates and he has reach advantage. He shall surprise Della Maddalena by TKO R3. This could go either way but Della Maddalena is going to run for a decision for sure. He won't be exchanging with Prates.

Dariush vs. Salkilld
Quillan Salkilld, TKO, R2
Age advantage & slight reach advantage for Salkilld. Lock that in. Dariush is getting TKoed again.

Elliott vs. Erceg
Tim Elliott, DEC
Erceg has age advantage and slight range advantage but I still think Elliott is better and he fights much tougher opponents. Late title run for Elliott in progress. *underdog*

Tafa vs. Christian
Kevin Christian, SUB, R2
Free win for Christian by submission. I don't think Christian is fraud even if he fights easy opponents.

Malkoun vs. Meerschaert
Jacob Malkoun, TKO, R1
Age advantage for Malkoun, this win should bring Meerschaert into retirement with his 5th loss in a row. Lock-in that pick.

Rowston vs. Bryczek
Cam Rowston, TKO, R1
Rowston has age advantage and he's a beast. It's going to be his 6th fight in less than 18 months. How about a 4 TKO win in a row? I'd like to see a R1 finish here.

Steele vs. Mar Fan
Dom Mar Fan, DEC
Mar Fan has age advantage. 4th fight in less than 12 months for him. What a true warrior. Mar Fan by decision. Time to find out if he can be a potential GOAT.

Micallef vs. Gorimbo
Jonathan Micallef, SUB, R2
Micallef has age advantage. This pick looks like a lock-in fight. Micallef by submission R2.

Thicknesse vs. Morales
Colby Thicknesse, DEC
Age advantage for Thicknesse.  Thicknesse has never never been finished. Thicknesse by decision.

Schultz vs. Johnston
Wes Schultz, SUB, R2
Age advantage for Schultz, Schultz only fight top opponent, the best of the best. Johnston has not fought in over 2 years. No shame here to go with Schultz by submission R2. This fight should end by a finish one way or another. Don't expect a decision. *Underdog*

posts.author: Mazeflare
posts.content: Marcos Degli is offering to step in to replace Jenkins and fight Rahiki at 145, that is crazy, I love Degli, he is galaxies above when it comes to muay thai, Rahiki its just a wild brawler right now ... but Degli is also a small 125, I feel like this would be  the equivalent of Uran Satybaldiev stepping in to fight Martin Buday, you could see one much more athletical and technical than the other but it didn't matter in the end, one too small to have his power be meaningful.
